title: Amylocarpus formosus
intro: Amylocarpus formosus, also known as the Formosa starch tree or Formosa amylocarpus, is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its large, starchy fruit. The fruit of Amylocarpus formosus has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include amylose, starch, and various alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Amylocarpus formosus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Amylocarpus formosus is a genus of plants in the family Achariaceae. This genus is native to Central and South America and is part of the order Malpighiales. The classification of Amylocarpus formosus is within the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the phylum Angiospermophyta and class Magnoliopsida. Amylocarpus formosus is further classified as a flowering plant.
